title: ProcScript for Data Validation
text: ProcScript for Data Validation ProcScript instructions can be used to explicitly validate data, set validation conditions, check modification and validation status, and handle errors. ProcScript Statements for Data Validation You can start validation for various object levels by the following ProcScript statements. Table 1. ProcScript Statements for Data Validation ProcScript Statement Action Scope Validate Validate all data marked as modified. Component entities validate/e Validate modified occurrences of an entity, and its inner entities Entity validate/o Validate the current occurrence of an entity and its inner entities Occurrence, including inner entities validateocc Validate an occurrence Occurrence, excluding inner entities validatekey Validate a key of the current occurrence. Key validatefield Validate a field of the current occurrence. Field Validation Status Functions Validation status functions enable you to check whether or not an object requires validation. They are set when data is modified and reset after the data has been validated; they cannot be set by ProcScript. $fieldvalidation $keyvalidation $occvalidation $instancevalidation For more information, see Validation and Modification Flags . Modification Status Functions Modification functions enable you to check the modification status of an object. Validating an object does not reset any of these modification functions. The modification status is reset only on the completion of a store process. $fieldmod $fielddbmod $keymod $occmod $occdbmod $formmod (deprecated) $formdbmod (deprecated) $instancemod $instancedbmod Validation Request Functions There are situations where data can be new (not stored in the database) but validation flags are not set on. This often occurs when initial values are declared for a field in its Initial Value property, or assigned in ProcScript using /init (for example, FLD1/init= Value ). Initial values are not taken into consideration for validation because they are not regarded as real data. To validate an initial value, you must explicitly request data validation for the field. The following ProcScript functions can be set to TRUE to force the validation of an object, regardless of the modification or validation status. $fieldcheck $keycheck $occcheck Setting these functions ensures that the validation triggers fire at the next opportunity (for example, when a store statement is issued). Alternatively, you can use ProcScript statements to activate the validation triggers immediately. Note that $occheck implicitly also causes fields and keys of an occurrence to be checked and the occurrence's validate trigger fired. Similarly, when a key is validated, the fields comprising the key are also validated. Key Handling Functions The following ProcScript functions obtain information about keys, including primary keys, candidate keys and index fields. $curkey $totkeys $keyfields $keytype Related Links Validation and Modification Flags All fields, keys, and occurrences have validation and modification flags which are used to determine data should be validated or stored. In general, when the content of a field is changed, the associated validation and modification flags are set to TRUE. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
